title: Monsoon Gored-Gored Beef
description: A crispy, savory dish originating from the Ethiopian monsoon season, perfect for a lunch or dinner. The fermented berbere spice blend adds a tangy, slightly sour flavor.
instructions: Cut the beef flank steak into thin strips.
Mix flour, salt, black pepper, and coriander in a bowl.
Toss the beef strips in the flour mixture, shaking off excess.
Heat 1 tablespoon of oil in a large pan over medium-high heat.
Add the beef strips and cook for 2-3 minutes on each side, or until browned.
Remove the beef from the pan and set aside.
Heat the remaining 1 tablespoon of oil in the pan over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger to the pan and cook for 1 minute.
Stir in the berbere spice blend and cook for 1 minute.
Add the mustard paste to the pan and cook for 1 minute.
Return the beef to the pan and cook for an additional 2-3 minutes, or until coated in the sauce.
Serve hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired.
Note: Traditionally, gored-gored beef is served with injera bread.
If serving with injera, place the beef on a piece of injera and wrap it up to eat.
Alternatively, serve with rice or roasted vegetables.
